content: Immutable audit log in Postgres: dedicated append-only table; app role gets INSERT+SELECT only, REVOKE UPDATE/DELETE; belt-and-braces BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE trigger raising exception; app must not own the table or connect as superuser. Tamper evidence: hash chain - each row stores sha256(serialized payload || prev_hash); optionally anchor periodic chain roots externally. Write path: audit writes for security-relevant events should be synchronous in the same transaction as the domain change (atomicity - no event without audit row); async listeners acceptable only for low-stakes usage analytics where loss is tolerable. Retention via partitioning by month + archival, never in-place delete.
